title: Gomphandra parviflora
taxonomy: Gomphandra parviflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Stemonuraceae. It belongs to the genus Gomphandra, which is a group of tropical plants native to Southeast Asia. The plant is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree, commonly found in shaded areas. The classification of Gomphandra parviflora is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, order - Aquifoliales, family - Stemonuraceae, genus - Gomphandra, and species - G. parviflora. The species is also known by its synonyms, including Gomphandra elliptica and Gomphandra nervosa.
uses: Gomphandra parviflora, a plant native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and bark are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In traditional medicine, Gomphandra parviflora is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential in treating conditions such as diabetes and cancer. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Gomphandra parviflora as a medicinal plant.
